data.Description: This study focuses on fish species detection and classification in the Arabian Gulf using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). The dataset consists of underwater images of seven fish species captured from videos in challenging conditions such as low luminosity, turbid water, and complex backgrounds. The research addresses issues like class imbalance, feature variance, and environmental noise by employing advanced optimization techniques and data augmentation strategies
data.Key Technologies:
a hybrid CNN approach combined with three hyperparameter optimization techniques:
Bayesian Optimization
Hyperband Optimization
Genetic Algorithm Optimization

Additionally, class weighting and data augmentation were applied to improve performance on imbalanced datasets
Deep Learning Frameworks:
The models were evaluated against a traditional CNN baseline.

metadata.searchable_text:
Red Sea Fish Classification Computer Vision This study focuses on fish species detection and classification in the Arabian Gulf using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). The dataset consists of underwater images of seven fish species captured from videos in challenging conditions such as low luminosity, turbid water, and complex backgrounds. The research addresses issues like class imbalance, feature variance, and environmental noise by employing advanced optimization techniques and data augmentation strategies  a hybrid CNN approach combined with three hyperparameter optimization techniques:
Bayesian Optimization
Hyperband Optimization
Genetic Algorithm Optimization

Additionally, class weighting and data augmentation were applied to improve performance on imbalanced datasets
Deep Learning Frameworks:
The models were evaluated against a traditional CNN baseline.

data.Description:
The Problem Statment:
first project in data scientist: we're going to take a look at the number of traffic accidents and driving licenses issued in Saudi Arabia. We'll seek to identify trends in the data and combine our data analysis with outside research to identify likely factors influencing the outcomes of traffic accidents in the various regions in Saudi Arabia.
Datasets:
Traffic Accidents dataFrame
Driving License dataframe
Accidents Details 2016 and 2017 dataframes
data.Key Technologies:
basic statistics (distributions, confidence intervals, hypothesis testing)
many Python programming concepts
visualizations
EDA

data.Description:
In the past years real estate Trades Prices in Japan's have been rising up and down,affected by the economic market. The main challenge here is to determine the future value of the property after a certain period of time, taking into account various expected factors such as the effects of the current pandemic to the future results.

We will build a time series forecasting model to forecast the property average prices based on historical data of all the 47 prefectures in Japan. Using time series Algorithms will help us to understand the characteristics and the statistics of the real estate data.

This work is organize to 3 notebook files:

Notebook 1: EDA & Feature Engineering.
Notebook 2: Static Modeling for Producing Time Series.
Notebook 2: Mean-Point Time Series Modeling.

using Tokyo prefecture data provided by by Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism of Japan (MLIT) with addition to the housing consumer price index for japan data, we introduce two approaches:

First Static Modeling for Producing Time Series:

For every quarter we produced static models. Then predict the price of an instance, using all quarter models. The result is a predicted time series data which will then be forecasted into the future using an LSTM without the timestamp features ['Year','Quarter'].

Second Mean-Point Time Series Modeling:

The pipeline of this approach is built to take an instance X, and produce a time series output from the full dataset that best represent X with the Mean Point Scheme Function. The time series data is then forecasted using baseline models ARIMAX & SARIMAX and the LSTM model.

data.Description:
Solve manual dates grading in factories, by building an CV model to detect the grade (quality) of dates for three types (Ajwah, Mabroom, Sukkary)  based on key features: Key features analyzed include color (using RGB values), size (via least-squares fitting ellipse), and texture (using Weber local descriptor) 
Three classifiers were tested: k-nearest neighbor (KNN), support vector machine (SVM), and convolutional neural network (CNN).

Three classifiers were tested: k-nearest neighbor (KNN), support vector machine (SVM), and convolutional neural network (CNN). KNN, SVM, and CNN models.
